theme: "ECHOES OF TROY: The Fading Light of Aphrodite's Flame - A mythic realm where the gods have grown weary, their powers waning as they navigate the mortal world with increasing irrelevance. Amidst this backdrop, a young demigod named Euphemus must master his own unpredictable fate, guided by the whispers of forgotten prophecies and the enigmatic smile of Aphrodite's fading presence."
visual_concept: "Visualize a haunting, sunset-lit scene in ancient Troy: crumbling city walls stretch towards a fiery horizon, while in the foreground, Euphemus stands alone, silhouetted against the warm glow. His eyes are downcast, lost in thought, as he grasps a worn wooden scroll with tattered edges, upon which Aphrodite's fading flames seem to dance. The air is thick with smoke from distant wars, and the once-vibrant colors of the city have dulled, reflecting the gods' own dwindling powers. In the background, subtle hints of forgotten prophecies whispered by ancient sages - crumbling stone pedestals, cryptic frescoes, and shattered mirrors reflecting the fading light."
character_context: "Euphemus, a 25-year-old demigod son of Aphrodite and a minor sea god, stands at the precipice of his destiny. His eyes, once bright with hope, now burn with an unquenchable fire as he struggles to reconcile his divine heritage with the harsh realities of mortal life. Orphaned at a young age, Euphemus has grown up amidst the ravages of war-torn Troy, eking out a meager existence on the city's fringes. Despite his parentage, he feels an uncanny sense of detachment from both the divine and human worlds. With Aphrodite's influence waning, Euphemus is left to navigate the treacherous landscape of fate, driven by an insatiable hunger for purpose and connection."
overlay_text: Aphrodite's flames aren't my only debt
caption_hook: When your gods have better credit scores than you
post_body: Aphrodite's flames may be fading, but my student loans aren't.
share_cta: Send this to the friend who's drowning in student loans
first_comment: Same, but instead of a prophecy, I got stuck with a 401(k) plan
hook_score_reason: This hook is short, memorable, and ties in with the overlay text. The joke is relatable to modern pain, and the CTA encourages sharing.
selection_reason: This candidate has a strong hook that resonates with relatable modern pain, and the overlay text is concise. The scene anchor adds a specific visual detail that ties in with Euphem
